The '''''Nocturnô''''' was an unfinished prototype of a [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], a class of [[Moon/Legends|moon]]-sized [[Battle station/Legends|battle stations]] armed with a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]-destroying [[Concave Dish Composite Beam Superlaser|superlaser]].

==History==
It was never fully completed, and concealed by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in low orbit around a gas-giant in an unknown system. At some point, apparently in the years between the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] and the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the female shapeshifter known as the [[Hydra (shapeshifter)|Hydra]] took the place of the ''Nocturnô''{{'s}} commander, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Tarkhan (Human)|Tarkhan]], and attempted to seize control of the battlemoon for her own nefarious purposes.<ref name="CB99" />

The Imperial forces assigned to the station seem to have served primarily as a protective garrison, including an extensive array of security droids, plus a force of elite [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] in black armor, who spent much of their time in cryopreservation cabinets, and were no longer entirely sane after [[Standard year/Legends|years]] serving aboard the station. The garrison also had some light armor, including [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|scout walkers]], which—perhaps in a manifestation of their state of mind—they stationed inside the docking bays.<ref name="CB99" />

The habitable areas aboard the ''Nocturnô'' were distinguished from those of the other Death Stars by somber lighting and an air of neglect, but although incomplete, the station did boast a number of [[Hangar/Legends|docking bays]], powerful [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]], and [[Detention area|detention blocks]] similar to those of her sisters, and a functional [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] prime weapon. The main generator appears to have been deactivated, but the Hydra brought with her a stolen Imperial energy-source capable of rendering the superlaser fully armed and operational.<ref name="CB99" />

However, there is no indication that the psychopathic [[Changeling/Legends|shapeshifter]] was ever allowed to make any effective use of the battlemoon's planet-cracking firepower; although direct evidence is lacking, it seems likely that the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] team who arrived at the same time prevented her plans, but whether the ''Nocturnô'' was actually destroyed in the process remains unknown.<ref name="CB99" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Nocturnô'' appears only in the French-language RPG magazine ''[[Casus Belli 99]]''; as a result, its canonical status is unclear.

The scout walkers carried aboard the battlemoon are identified in French by the designation TR-TT or ''Transport de Reconnaissance Tout Terrain''. At the time the magazine was written, this designation indicated the [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]]; but some French fansites claim that the same designation is shared by the smaller, older vehicle known in English-language material as the [[All Terrain Recon Transport/Legends|AT-RT]]. [http://www.starwars-holonet.com/holonet.php?fiche=ship_atrt] [http://www.swg1.net/encyclo/voir_fiche_in.php?fiche=254].

In French, the words "Reconnaissance" and "Scout" can only be translated by a single word : "Reconnaissance" (others translations are less adequate, like "Observation" or "Exploration"). The AT-S(scout)T is translated officially into TR-TT, and AT-R(reconnaissance)T must also be translated into "TR-TT" - or "Clone Army TR-TT". However, the ''Transport de Reconnaissance Tout Terrain'' designate in French the Imperial model of TR-TT (All Terrain Scout Transport), not the Grand Army one (All Terrain Reconnaissance Transport).
